content: A spark blossoms at the wand-tip and sheds a calm white light, banishing shadow without warmth.
The wand-light folds back into the wood; darkness rushes in to fill the space.
The named object lurches across the chamber as though pulled by an invisible thread.
A bolt of red sparks knocks the opponent's wand clean out of their grip.
A shimmering dome of force forms in front of the caster, turning curses aside.
The lock clicks as if it had merely been waiting for someone to ask politely.
The most recent memory blurs at the edges, fades, and is gone before the witness can name it.
The chosen object lifts as if the weight of it had simply been forgotten.
A silver beast leaps from the wand-tip, shaped from the caster's brightest memory.
Every limb stiffens; the body falls in one rigid piece, a statue carved of breath.
A jet of red light catches the target square in the chest; consciousness leaves them like a snuffed candle.
The shards of the broken thing turn in air, find their old neighbors, and fuse.
Charms of concealment thin and lift, showing what was hidden underneath.
Voice, footstep, and the hum of an instrument all alike fall mute.
The target's certainty slips out from under them; one thought refuses to follow another.
The obstacle bursts apart in a spray of dust and small bright sparks.
Confronted with absurdity, the boggart laughs itself into mist.
Surface thoughts flicker behind the target's eyes; a discourtesy without consent.
A secret is folded into the soul of a chosen confidant; without their telling, it is invisible to the world.
A small object glows blue, then settles — a one-use portkey to a place fixed in mind.
The caster vanishes with a small thunderclap and re-forms a great distance away.
A wand is forced to repeat its last several castings as glimmering echoes in the air.
The candle becomes a bonfire; whole halls bleach to white in a single instant.
The stunned target stirs and wakes, color returning to their cheeks.
The object thins, fades, and is gone — no destination implied, no remains.
A second copy of the object appears, flawless to the eye and crumbling at the first touch.
The wand draws a glowing rune in the air; the line lingers for hours, marking what mattered.
A ward is set; if an enemy crosses the marked threshold, the caster is alerted at any distance.
Liquid lifts cleanly from cloth, parchment, or skin and is gone before it can stain.
Tells the caster precisely what charms or potions have been laid upon the object in question.
Crimson-scaled wyrm of the volcanic ranges; its breath cooks armor before it touches skin.
Marsh-dwelling dragon whose acidic breath fells whole groves in a single exhalation.
Mountain-peak dragon of frostbreath and fairer counsel; sometimes consents to speak with mortals.
Sun-scaled wyrm whose breath is light made solid; rare, ancient, and not to be hunted.
Silver-white horse with a single spiral horn; its blood grants half a life and curses the drinker.
Crimson and gold bird that lights itself on fire upon dying, rising again from the ash.
Eagle-headed lion of the high mountains; fiercely loyal to a chosen rider, lethal otherwise.
Half-eagle, half-horse; demands a courteous bow before it will permit approach.
Half-human, half-horse; gifted archers and stargazers who keep aloof from human affairs.
King of serpents; its gaze petrifies, its venom dissolves stone, its fangs cut anything that bleeds.
A cursed human who turns to a wolf-shape under the full moon; mind goes with the change.
A noble of the undead; centuries of cunning packed behind a smile that does not reach the eyes.
Hill-sized brute with regenerative flesh; its skin hardens to stone in cold winds.
Knee-high tinker-thief of the deep woods; treasure-mad, easily frightened by silver bells.
Desert-dwelling orcish raider; rides giant lizards and reads tracks weeks old in dry sand.
Riverine cousin of the sea-siren; lures wading travelers with songs about dry beds.
Salt-feathered harpy of the cliff colonies; opportunistic, foul-tongued, fast in a stoop.
A drowned soul that crystallized in the brine; appears as a glittering figure stepping from the surf.
Undead warrior bound to its barrow; rises if grave-goods are disturbed, falls when restored.
Old Thalamir, who bottled his soul in a black gem and forgot which kingdom now houses it.
Spirit of the desert flame; bargains in three wishes, each more carefully phrased than the last.
Hand-shaped servant of red clay; obeys the word inscribed upon its forehead and not one syllable more.
Eight feet of forged iron animated by a binding rune; tireless, slow, and remarkably hard to argue with.
Winged horse the color of fresh snow; will only carry the pure-hearted, by which it means the kind.
Sea-horse the size of a charger; tide-taught, salt-named, fond of pearls.
Flame-skinned lizard that dwells in the hearth; eats coal and exhales sparks when content.
Water-horse of the deep loch; offers a free ride and drowns its passenger in cold sport.
White-furred giant of the high passes; rarely seen, rarely hostile, occasionally generous with shelter.
Frostbitten cannibal of the deep northern woods; tall, gaunt, antlered, and patient as winter.
Eagle the size of a cottage; takes whole oxen back to its eyrie, sometimes a careless rider too.
Three-headed hound of the underworld gate; sleeps to soft music, not to bribes.
Marsh-serpent with five heads; for each one severed, two grow back unless the wound is cauterized.
Lion-bodied, scorpion-tailed, human-faced; all hunger and red-handed wit.
Shapeless horror of the cold deeps; cyclopean, wrong, and best left unwatched.
Red-eyed harbinger glimpsed before disasters; never harms, never fails to herald.
Seal-folk who shed their skin to walk among us; if their skin is taken, so is their freedom.
Tree-bound spirit of an oak or rowan; if the tree is felled, the dryad goes with it.
Spring-spirit of clear waters; her favor depends on the cleanliness of the source.
Graveyard-dwelling cannibal; weak in daylight, ravenous after dusk, terrified of bells.
Keening spirit whose wail foretells a near death in the household she haunts.
Tentacled leviathan of the deep ocean; sinks ships not from malice but from curiosity.
Sea-singer who lures sailors to wreck on rocks polished smooth with bones.
Bull-headed brute who haunts a labyrinth; was once a man, remembers most of it.
Serpent-haired gorgon whose gaze turns flesh to stone; sees herself only by polished shield.
Lion-goat-serpent in one body; three appetites, one breath of fire, no patience.
Mountain-shouldered beast that breaks armies by walking through them.
Ocean-spanning serpent older than continents; surfacing once a generation to take a breath.
Shape-shifter that prefers the form of a treasure chest; springs upon those who reach inside.
Antlered hare of campfire tales; tricksterish, fast, and harder to catch than a story.
Swamp-dweller of antipodean legend; bellows like a cow, eats like a wolf.
Black tower at the edge of the world, where the last archmage watches the slow unraveling of the weave.
A clearing in the deep wood where the trees murmur secrets to anyone who will sit still and listen.
Sunken cathedral whose bells still toll once a day beneath sixty fathoms of cold sea.
Cliffside observatory tracking comets, the slow death of stars, and the dreams of distant gods.
Hidden bazaar in the bones of a slain volcano; deals are sealed in fire and never quite forgotten.
Yew wand with a single phoenix tail-feather core; crackles audibly when angered.
Holly wand wound around a strand of dragon heartstring; powerful but temperamental.
Ebony wand with unicorn-hair core; reliable, patient, refuses to cast unforgivables.
The elder wand: legendary, undefeated, and inevitably won from its previous master.
True cloak of invisibility; not faded by use, not seen by enchantment, woven of demiguise hair.
Cloak of dragon-hide; turns aside flame, sword, and most lesser hexes.
Cloak the color of a moonless sky; takes on the shape of shadows it stands beside.
Sailor's cloak woven with kelpie-hair; sheds water entirely and warms in cold spray.
Plain wool cloak with three deep pockets; mending charms have been laid into the seams.
Smooth black stone; turn it three times in the hand and the dead return as half-present shades.
Polished obsidian disc; if held to the eye in moonlight, shows the next thing the holder must do.
Pale orange stone that warms the palm at noon and cools by midnight; gives strength under sky.
Cool blue-white stone that absorbs moonlight and releases it as a steady glow.
Black iron dagger that drinks the wounds it makes; an evil thing in honest hands.
Silver dagger with a thorned hilt that bleeds the wielder for power; balanced, vicious, costly.
Knife shaped from a young dragon's fang; cuts armor as if it were soft cheese.
Slender stiletto with a poisoned reservoir in the hilt; refills overnight if left in moonlight.
Black-glass knife used for ritual cuts and for nothing else; chips if used in violence.
Longsword whose blade ignites at the wielder's command; cools to ordinary steel between breaths.
Pale steel longsword that frosts the air around it; freezes blood in shallow wounds.
Longsword forged by a paladin's vow; sings in the presence of fiends, blunt against the innocent.
Wraith-blade able to cut intangible foes as readily as solid ones; cold to the grip.
Heavy war-axe of dwarven make; the head is engraved with the name of the first foe it bit.
Crude orcish cleaver, ugly and effective; the spine still has a tooth from its last grudge.
Pale ash longbow strung with mooncord; arrows leave it without sound and arrive without warning.
Black-iron crossbow whose bolts burn with a cold dark fire; not for indoor use.
Quiver of twelve arrows, each enchanted to a different small trick: smoke, light, slip, fetch.
Common red potion that knits shallow wounds and steadies the heart; tastes of cherries.
Cool blue potion that restores spent magical reserves; tastes faintly of thunder and clean rain.
Quicksilver-bright potion that accelerates limb and thought for a quarter hour; the comedown is rough.
Thick amber potion smelling of iron filings; doubles the drinker's lift for a half-hour.
Gray-flecked draught that hardens skin against blow and blade for a few minutes.
Pale, shifting potion; the drinker fades from sight along with whatever they hold tightly.
Sludgy draught into which a hair of the imitated person has been stirred; one hour as them, exactly.
Liquid luck; the drinker's day goes irritatingly well in every plausible particular.
Pearlescent love-potion; smells different to every nose, always of what the drinker most desires.
Clear, odorless truth-serum; three drops are enough to compel a fortnight of confessions.
Smoking blue potion that lets a werewolf keep their wits through the change.
Black potion that puts the drinker into a sleep indistinguishable from death until counter-brewed.
Yellow essence of murtlap; soothes burns, raw flesh, and the prick of a hexed quill.
Iron-rich tonic for those who have lost blood; tastes how a copper coin smells.
Plain silver band warmed against curses; the wearer feels small hexes glance away.
Red-stoned ring that turns flame from harm to mere heat against the wearer's skin.
Pale blue ring; a touch from the wearer cools whatever it lights upon, by a little or by a lot.
Black-iron ring that casts a small pool of dimness around the wearer at all times.
Crystal-stoned ring that pierces illusion, glamour, and most charm-work.
Plain pewter ring that turns away surface legilimency; will not stop a determined intruder.
Silver ring that knits small wounds slowly; the wearer ages a fraction faster for the favor.
Signet ring of the moonhouse; bearing it admits the wearer to certain doors that admit no one else.
Carved bone amulet inscribed with three wards; trembles warm against the chest before harm strikes.
Smoked-glass amulet; if held while sleeping, gives a true dream once a month.
Silver amulet that lets the wearer be understood in any tongue, though they cannot reply in kind.
Yellowed bone amulet; bones do not break under its wearer, however hard the fall.
Leather-bound tome of summoning circles; pages turn themselves when a marked rite is begun.
Black tome of necromantic rites; the cover sweats faintly and the index reorders by reader's intent.
Heavy red tome of fire and force evocations; the spine grows warm during practice.
Iron-bound tome of binding sigils, chains, and contracts; the lock will not yield to violence.
Slim, golden-leafed tome detailing the proper phrasing of fey bargains and the cost of every comma.
Liber Primus, the first book; opens for one reader at a time and remembers each of them.
Forbidden tome of names that should not be spoken; bound in a hide of unknown provenance.
Polished crystal ball on a brass stand; clears for divination in the right hands, fogs for the wrong.
Tall mirror in a gilded frame; shows the viewer their deepest unspoken longing.
Round mirror that shows the wielder's enemies — distant blurs that sharpen as they approach.
Glass spinning-top that whistles and lights up in the presence of treachery near at hand.
Glass orb that turns red when the bearer has forgotten something they meant to do.
Acid-green quill that paraphrases speech as it writes — sometimes flatteringly, sometimes ruinously.
Reliable charmed quill that never needs an inkwell; ink is conjured a drop at a time.
Empty gilt frame whose former subject occasionally returns to scold passersby in a dead language.
Brass lantern whose flame does not gutter in wind, rain, or breath of magic.
Tin lantern whose light only reveals what its bearer has already lost; useful sometimes.
Brass compass with no cardinal markings; the needle points always toward the bearer's chosen home.
Silver compass whose needle points toward the most honest person within a mile.
Tarnished brass spyglass; sees stars as they were a thousand years ago, never as they are.
Plain leather satchel that holds whatever is put into it without weight or apparent end.
Common adventurer's bag of holding; one cubic yard inside a quart's outside.
Heavy chest of dark wood with an orichalcum lock; rumored to refuse opening if the contents would harm.
Carved wooden cube that becomes a different shape each time it is solved; requires solving twelve times to open.
Marble chess set whose pieces argue with their player about which moves are tactically correct.
Worn deck of cards that lightly explodes when matched in a pair; popular at parties, banned at funerals.
Hand-painted deck whose figures move between cards when no one is watching.
Old trunk that holds a sleeping boggart; opens only for those whose worst fear it cannot reach.
Small cauldron that stirs itself counterclockwise on command; popular with brewing students.
Standard schoolroom brass cauldron; reliable for most beginner potions, scorches at the boundary.
Light pewter cauldron favored by traveling brewers; tarnishes spectacularly with corrosive draughts.
Showpiece golden cauldron used only for the rarest potions; brews at half-temperature.
An ordinary-looking wooden duck enchanted as a portkey; quacks once before activation.
An old left boot, deeply rusted at the eyelets; carries one party once to a precise destination.
Velvet pouch of bright green floo powder; speak the destination clearly or be swept somewhere wrong.
Top-of-line racing broom; mahogany handle, iron-hard birch twigs, vain about its turns.
Last decade's flagship broom; still excellent, slightly out of fashion, beloved of nostalgic fliers.
Single-use scroll of an evocation fireball; the parchment turns to ash on casting.
Hourglass in a silver frame; its sand falls always at true time, whatever local clocks insist.
Cracked golden time-turner; fragments still spin, but no longer reliably backward.
Steel chisel that cuts running water as easily as wood; favored of itinerant rune-cutters.
Round palm-sized stone that warms before fair weather and cools before storms.
Brass doorknob attachable to any door; once attached, the door also opens onto another door of the maker's choice.
Small framed pane of glass; mounted to a wall, it shows a distant garden the maker once loved.
Brass key whose teeth shift to fit any common lock; complains audibly when forced.
Small crystal vial used to store a single memory drawn out by wand-tip; corked with cedar.
Quill made from a single phoenix tail-feather; never blots, signs the writer's name in rare red ink.
